metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the single multi-file `grep -q` in AC6 with three separate assertions so each file is independently verified (e.g. `grep -q 'your-first-change' CONTRIBUTING.md && grep -q 'your-first-change' docs/README.md && grep -q 'your-first-change' .github/pull_request_template.md`).
findings.finding: AC6's proving command `grep -q "your-first-change" CONTRIBUTING.md docs/README.md .github/pull_request_template.md` silently passes if only ONE of the three files contains the string, because `grep -q` with multiple files exits 0 as soon as the first match is found — the remaining files are never checked.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the single `grep -q` multi-file invocation in AC6 with three separate per-file checks (e.g. `grep -q "your-first-change" CONTRIBUTING.md && grep -q "your-first-change" docs/README.md && grep -q "your-first-change" .github/pull_request_template.md`) so each file is independently verified.
findings.finding: AC6's proof command `grep -q "your-first-change" CONTRIBUTING.md docs/README.md .github/pull_request_template.md` uses POSIX grep -q across three files in a single invocation. The -q flag makes grep exit 0 on the FIRST match found in ANY of the three files (short-circuit exit), so if one or two target files are missing the link, the command still exits 0 — silently falsely passing the funnel-links criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (Funnel links present — prove: `grep -q "your-first-change" CONTRIBUTING.md docs/README.md .github/pull_request_template.md`)
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace AC6 proof with:
```
grep -q "your-first-change" CONTRIBUTING.md \
  && grep -q "your-first-change" docs/README.md \
  && grep -q "your-first-change" .github/pull_request_template.md
```
Or equivalently: `for f in CONTRIBUTING.md docs/README.md .github/pull_request_template.md; do grep -q 'your-first-change' "$f" || { echo "MISSING in $f"; exit 1; }; done`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add grep anchors for the four unproven beats in AC1, e.g.: `grep -q 'OAuth\|sign.in\|HTTP Credentials\|/a/' docs/your-first-change.md` for account+credential+clone, and `grep -q 'branch\|checkout' docs/your-first-change.md` for the branch beat.
findings.finding: AC1 asserts coverage of all nine beats but only five of them (push-refs, hook, amend, signed-commit, trailer) have grep anchors; the remaining four beats — account setup, credential generation, clone, and branch — have no mechanical proof, so the nine-beat claim is only partially verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete grep (or `grep -c = 0` for negative claims) to AC3, AC4, and AC5 — e.g. for AC4: `grep -q 'maintainer.*[Ss]ubmit\|[Ss]ubmit.*maintainer' docs/your-first-change.md`; for AC5: `grep -cE 'coverage.gap.sub|coverage reason' docs/your-first-change.md` = 0.
findings.finding: AC3 ('outsider ticket flow is fully described'), AC4 ('doc states a maintainer performs Submit'), and AC5 ('doc does not enumerate coverage-gap sub-reasons') each carry the word 'prove:' implicitly but provide no concrete grep or deterministic command — they rely on human prose inspection, making them non-machine-verifiable.

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the story (or narrow the subtitle) to reflect actual scope — e.g., 'Outsider onboarding: your-first-change tutorial + funnel links' — so the title matches the body's explicit OUT-scoping of CONTRIBUTING restructure and README front door.
findings.finding: The plan title promises 'CONTRIBUTING restructure' and 'README front door' as deliverables of this story, but the body explicitly scopes both OUT ('no restructuring', 'README front door: own story'). A reader acting on the title would expect structural CONTRIBUTING.md work and a README rewrite that never arrive, causing intent mismatch and potential duplicate work in sibling stories.
findings.location: Plan title vs. Scope section (OUT bullet: 'README.md front-door work (own story: README front door)'; 'no restructuring')
findings.suggested_fix: Change the story title to: 'Outsider onboarding: your-first-change tutorial, funnel links (CONTRIBUTING top-link, docs ToC, PR template)' — this names the three actual deliverables without implying the two out-of-scope sibling stories.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the nine-beat sequence (and AC1) that the tutorial must clarify the two-phase commit flow: commit first (with `-s`, without trailer if the ID isn't yet known), receive ticket ID via maintainer reply, then `git commit --amend` to add the trailer before pushing — so the async wait does not block the outsider at an undocumented step.
findings.finding: The nine-beat sequence in the plan lists 'branch → signed commit w/ trailer' as a single step, but the outsider ticket flow is inherently async: the outsider must open a GitHub issue and WAIT for a maintainer to reply with the rebar ticket ID before the trailer can be formed. If the tutorial encodes the beat as 'commit with both -s AND rebar-ticket: in one shot,' a reader who starts coding before receiving the ticket ID will either stall at commit time or push a commit that fails CI's Verified gate (missing trailer). The plan does not specify that the tutorial must make clear that committing without the trailer first, then amending after the ID arrives, is the expected flow — leaving this gap to the implementer risks a confusingly-ordered tutorial.
findings.location: What §3 (Your first change beat sequence) and AC1 ('nine-beat sequence: … branch → signed commit w/ trailer → push refs/for')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a half-beat between 'signed commit' and 'push refs/for': 'if you don't yet have your ticket ID, commit with -s now; once the maintainer replies with the ID, `git commit --amend` to add the `rebar-ticket:` trailer before pushing.' Update AC1's grep proof to also require `--amend` appears in the context of adding the trailer (not only for responding to review findings).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion covering the gotchas table: verify it exists (e.g., grep for `| missing Change-Id |` or a section heading) and contains at minimum the seven entries enumerated in What §4.
findings.finding: The gotchas table (described in 'What' §4 with seven specific entries: missing Change-Id; pushed to main instead of refs/for/main; new commit instead of amend; missing sign-off; missing/unresolvable rebar-ticket trailer; CI flake; OAuth/hook setup failures) is a concrete described deliverable with no covering acceptance criterion — an implementer could omit or stub it with no AC violation.
findings.location: What section 4 (gotchas table) vs Acceptance Criteria (all six lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: '- [ ] Gotchas table present — prove: `grep -qE "Change-Id|refs/for/main|amend|sign-off|rebar-ticket|OAuth" docs/your-first-change.md` returns hits for all six distinct gotcha patterns, and the doc contains a markdown table (prose or table) with ≥ 7 rows covering the enumerated failure states.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion or extend AC line 1 to verify the see-also back-link: `grep -qi 'CONTRIBUTING' docs/your-first-change.md` (ensuring the tutorial references CONTRIBUTING.md as the full reference).
findings.finding: The 'See-also' section (described in 'What' §5 as 'See-also → CONTRIBUTING.md as the full reference') is a described deliverable with no covering acceptance criterion — it could be omitted without failing any AC.
findings.location: What section 5 (See-also) vs Acceptance Criteria (all six lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC line 1's grep list to include `CONTRIBUTING` or add a dedicated AC: '- [ ] See-also present — prove: `grep -q "CONTRIBUTING" docs/your-first-change.md`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` (and `feature_request.yml`) should receive direct `your-first-change` links alongside the PR template treatment; if yes, add them to Scope and AC6; if intentionally excluded, add a brief rationale (e.g., 'Issues are disabled so the contact-link route is secondary').
findings.finding: `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` contains a dedicated '🛠️ Contribute a change (via Gerrit)' contact link pointing to CONTRIBUTING.md — it is a named contributor-funnel file outside the artifact's directory that the plan does not enumerate in Scope, What, or AC6. When CONTRIBUTING.md gains the top-of-file tutorial link, this route still works one click away; but the config.yml is the only GitHub Issues entry point for would-be contributors and is as much a 'first contact' surface as the PR template. The plan calls out `.github/pull_request_template.md` as a first-contact surface but omits this parallel one.
findings.location: Scope / What section (funnel links) / AC6 grep command
findings.suggested_fix: Add `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` to the Scope IN list and the AC6 grep, OR add a parenthetical in the Scope section: '`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` intentionally omitted — Issues are disabled on the mirror, making this a low-traffic path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an 'abandoning a change' gotcha row (or a brief note in the flow) explaining how to abandon an open Gerrit review (UI: Change → Abandon, or `git push origin HEAD:refs/for/main%abandon`) so the tutorial covers the exit path when a contributor decides not to proceed.
findings.finding: The tutorial's flow exit points cover the success path (both votes green → maintainer submits) and several error/retry paths, but the abandon exit is unspecified: a first-time contributor who pushes a change to Gerrit and then decides not to continue leaves an open change that remains visible to maintainers indefinitely. Gerrit requires an explicit 'Abandon' action; no tutorial guidance exists for this Gerrit-specific cleanup step.
findings.location: What section, beat 3 ('Your first change') and Gotchas table
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Gotchas table: 'Changed your mind after pushing? Open the change in the Gerrit UI → kebab menu → Abandon, and leave a short comment. This closes the review cleanly for maintainers.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
